title: RAG System Design
text: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) improves factual accuracy by grounding model responses in retrieved context. Production RAG systems separate ingestion, indexing, retrieval, reranking, and generation. Chunking strategy strongly affects recall and latency.

title: Chunking Best Practices
text: Fixed-size chunking is simple and fast but can split concepts. Semantic-aware chunking preserves coherence by splitting on headings and sentence boundaries. Overlap mitigates boundary loss but increases index size and retrieval latency.

title: Hybrid Retrieval
text: Sparse retrieval such as BM25 excels on exact keywords. Dense retrieval captures semantic similarity. Hybrid retrieval combines both scores to improve recall across ambiguous or paraphrased questions.

title: Reranking in RAG
text: A reranker receives candidate passages and reorders them by query relevance. Even lightweight lexical reranking can improve answer quality when the initial retriever returns noisy passages.

title: Latency and Throughput
text: Low latency requires efficient retrieval and bounded context windows. Limiting top-k, reducing chunk length, and using quantized local models help maintain fast response times on consumer hardware.

title: MLX Inference
text: MLX enables efficient local inference on Apple Silicon. Quantized model checkpoints often trade slight quality loss for significant speed and memory improvements.

title: vLLM Inference
text: vLLM serves LLMs with high-throughput batching and paged attention. It is commonly used in production APIs where concurrent requests require stable latency under load.

title: Monitoring RAG
text: Production monitoring should track answer faithfulness, retrieval hit rate, latency p50 and p95, token usage, and error rate. These metrics enable data-driven tuning of RAG pipelines.
